danger_signals.visual_form: Skull-like symbols (North American version); Stars of David (Japanese version)
danger_signals.meaning: Source of spawning enemies; threat to civilization growth; must be sealed to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated creatures spawning continuously from lair locations
danger_signals.meaning: Persistent environmental threat; impede civilization progression; appear as obstacles in world

danger_signals.visual_form: Large mythological/religious-themed creatures (e.g., manticore in Bloodpool)
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat requiring direct confrontation; represent lieutenants of Tanzra; visual manifestation of evil occupation
Instant death threat in platforming sections; world corruption/danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Implied through simulation mechanics; people under evil influence
danger_signals.meaning: Civilization degradation; moral corruption under Tanzra's rule

safety_signals.visual_form: Increasing population count in simulation mode; expansion of constructed structures
safety_signals.meaning: Civilization health; faith in The Master; world recovery

safety_signals.visual_form: Higher-tier buildings available after civilization level increases
safety_signals.meaning: Progress towards prosperity; sealing of monster lairs enables construction advancement
Threat elimination; regional purification; civilization advancement
The Master's protective guidance; divine support for civilization
Safe haven; divine realm; source of miracles

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Sky Palace on clouds; Angel companion; Divine miracles (lightning, rain, sunlight, wind, earthquakes)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Statue form (animated by Master's power during action sequences)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Temples built by civilization; prayer sites; civilized lands under Master's protection

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Monster Lairs; Six Lieutenants; Death Heim stronghold
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss creatures based on mythology/religion; flying monsters; transformed evil populace
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Six divided lands controlled by lieutenants; monster-infested regions; corrupted civilization

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Regional bosses; each controls one land division
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss-tier enemies with mythological themes (manticore in Bloodpool, etc.)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: One lieutenant per region; their defeat liberates that land

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: World divided into six lands; Monster Lairs present; corrupted populace; temples empty of faith
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Continuous monster spawning; civilization refuses to build; people turned to evil

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Angel appears; miracles become available; sky palace active
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Populace begins responding to Master's guidance; civilization growth initiates

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Population increases; basic structures appear; civilization level rises
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: More advanced structures buildable; people reclaim lands

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Skull/Star symbols disappear from map; regions cleared of flying monsters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No more spawning from sealed lair; population builds highest-tier structures available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Regional liberation; that land's visual corruption removed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Master's power increases (HP/SP gain); populace returns to faith

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Temples present but empty; civilizations peaceful and self-sufficient
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Populace no longer prays; faith abandoned; Master & Angel depart to heavens

environmental_storytelling.example: Simulation mode shows civilization state under Master's guardianship; action sequences represent The Master's physical confrontation of evil forces. Together they illustrate both spiritual guidance and militant opposition to Tanzra.
Four monster lairs visible at region start; their presence communicates active threat. Sealing them visually removes the danger symbol, communicating liberation and progress.

environmental_storytelling.visual_manifestation: Population growth directly tied to The Master's power recovery; world-state communication through demographic expansion

environmental_storytelling.example: Visible building tier progression in simulation mode; advanced structures only appear after lairs sealed, showing civilization advancement tied to danger elimination
Bosses reference real-world religion/mythology (Greek, Hindu); visual manifestation of Tanzra's corruption using recognizable dark spiritual imagery
Final revelation shows temples built but unpopulated; communicates theme of lost faith and humanity's self-sufficiency, negating need for deity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Shoots arrows at monsters; indicates building locations; represents divine guidance
Platform-jumps; defeats monsters; confronts bosses; manifestation of Master's will in physical form
Continuous respawn; impede civilization progress; attack angel/statue
No longer spawn; eliminated from region
Guard end-stage action sequences; represent Tanzra's control; defeat triggers regional liberation
Refuse to build; diminished population; corrupted by Tanzra's lieutenants
Population grows; builds structures; advances civilization level

entity_behavioral_tells.visual_manifestation: Absent from world; Angel must act as proxy

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can cast miracles; populace responds; civilization expands

notes: ActRaiser communicates world-state primarily through dual gameplay modes: simulation visuals (population, structures, lair symbols) indicate civilization health and faith levels, while action sequences provide mythologically-themed visual representation of evil forces. The game's allegory for Judeo-Christian theology is embedded in visual design (sky palace, angel, temples, miracles, divine statue) despite Nintendo of America censorship removing explicit religious language. Monster Lair symbols changed from Stars of David to skulls for Western release, indicating localization of danger iconography. The ending's empty temples serve as final visual storytelling: faith abandoned through civilization self-sufficiency. World state progression is visually communicated through sealing mechanics (threat symbols disappearing) and architecture advancement (higher tiers unlocking).
sources_used: wikipedia - ActRaiser main article (Gameplay, Plot, Development/Religious subtext sections)
